title: The Marine Mammal Protection Act (P.L. 92522): Primer and Issues for Congress
summary: Congress passed the Marine Mammal Protection Act (MMPA) in 1972 (P.L. 92-522; 16 U.S.C. §§1361-1423h) to conserve marine mammal populations and protect them from extinction or depletion as a result of human activities. Since enactment, Congress has amended the MMPA at times and passed the most recent comprehensive amendments in 1994 (P.L. 103-238). The MMPA intersects with other marine-related legislation, such as...

title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.

title: What Does the G7 Do?
summary: The Group of Seven (G7) has been a forum to coordinate global policy for fifty years, but experts are increasingly questioning the group’s relevance.

title: As Death Toll Spikes, Venezuela’s Earthquakes Test U.S. Disaster Relief
summary: Two powerful earthquakes struck the northern coast of Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing homes and buildings. Without USAID, can the Trump administration deliver life-saving aid to the survivors?

title: From Washington, DC, to the States: Rethinking Head Start
summary: Social science research finds that Head Start, a federally funded preschool program for children from low-income families, does not prepare children for K–12 schools, nor does it improve their social skills. Reports on spending and safety also show that Head Start centers waste money and fail to protect children. After more than six decades and more than $240 billion in federal spending, Head Start is a poor option...

title: These Churches Are Putting Family Before Politics
summary: Close to 70 percent of Black children in America are born to unmarried parents . Nearly half live with a single mother . Both figures are significantly higher than the national average and reflect the steep decline in marriage among African Americans since the 1960s. But two Black churches are showing the way to address the decline of fatherhood and family. Their insights have relevance far outside the Black...

title: Europe’s Migration “Crackdown” Still Pulls Its Punches
summary: Europe’s migration crisis is no longer a border security problem. It is a crisis of sovereignty. The result has been exactly what many warned would happen: overwhelmed border systems, declining public...
